rationale: The round-3 mechanism itself is coherent: step-2.3 pending marker (post-2.25, pre-CommitAll), step-3.75 audit (post-ADR-gate, pre-close), fail-open override scoped to unsatisfied obligations, AppliedRefutations-grounded asymmetry stated identically in step 5 / Bead-2 AC / Bead-3 step 3; the message-superset design and AC10 are untouched (refutations never alter the leg-9.5 message). §8-vs-§6 is NOT a contradiction: fail-open targets absent panel evidence, while refutation_pending is affirmative evidence on the state authority; the plan scopes it precisely (§6 unchanged for no-obligation beads). F3-R2-3 ADDRESSED (fatal/warn/ignore taxonomy + grep step), minus a path typo. BUT the round-3 SPEC amendment left two internal contradictions: Out of Scope still says internal/bead is 'consumed as-is' while In Scope/Impacted Domains/R2 mandate the fail-closed edit; and ADR Touchpoints + AC6 still say '§6/§8 unchanged, re-affirmed' while R2(b) changes the fail-open path and the plan (operator-authorized) AMENDS §8. Two one-line spec fixes.
concrete_changes_required: spec.md Out of Scope: strike or rewrite the stale `internal/bead` bullet ('MergeMetadata is consumed as-is through the existing completeMergeMetadataFn seam') — it directly contradicts In Scope's `internal/bead/bdcli.go` fail-closed + GetMetadata entry, the amended Impacted Domains, and R2(b). Replace with e.g. 'internal/bead — no changes beyond the In-Scope fail-closed MergeMetadata + GetMetadata helper; the completeMergeMetadataFn seam itself is unchanged.'

spec.md ADR Touchpoints (the '§6 ... and §8 ... are unchanged and must be re-affirmed' sentence), the In Scope ADR-0037 bullet ('§6/§8 re-affirmed'), and AC6 ('re-affirming §6/§8'): reconcile with the round-3 design — §6 is re-affirmed WITH the recorded-obligation override R2(b) itself mandates, and §8 is AMENDED (operator-authorized narrow durable-refutation carve-out, posture otherwise re-affirmed), matching plan Bead-3 steps 3-4 and ADR Fitness.

plan.md Bead-2 step 6 blast-radius taxonomy: fix the path typo `internal/bead/repair.go:86` → `cmd/mindspec/repair.go:86` (the fatal guard.NewFailure caller lives in cmd/mindspec; verified). LOW — the class assignment is correct and the grep verification step would surface it anyway.

findings.summary: Spec internal contradiction left by the round-3 amendment: spec.md Out of Scope still lists 'internal/bead — MergeMetadata is consumed as-is through the existing completeMergeMetadataFn seam' (verified at 14fd3312), while In Scope lists 'internal/bead/bdcli.go (execution domain) — bead.MergeMetadata made fail-closed ... plus a GetMetadata read helper', Impacted Domains declares execution/internal/bead/** with the O3 amendment note, and R2(b) mandates the fail-closed change. The amendment note says it 'strikes the no-change-lands-there line' but only struck it in Impacted Domains; the Out of Scope bullet survived. Consequence: Bead 2's implementer/panel reads a spec that simultaneously requires and forbids the internal/bead edit — a reviewer applying Out of Scope literally would file a spurious RC against the required diff (which, under this very spec's regime, blocks). One-line spec fix.

Stale §6/§8 wording in the spec vs the round-3 design: ADR Touchpoints says '§6 (fail-open without a panel...) and §8 (trust boundary...) are unchanged and must be re-affirmed in the amendment text', echoed by In Scope ('§6/§8 re-affirmed') and AC6 ('re-affirming §6/§8'). But R2(b) — updated in this same round — mandates that the §6 fail-open path additionally CHECK bead metadata and refuse on an unsatisfied refutation_pending, and the plan (Bead-3 step 4, ADR Fitness) AMENDS §8 with the operator-authorized durable-refutation anti-tamper carve-out, explicitly 'not merely re-affirmed'. Substantively no semantic conflict (the durable-obligation content IS in R2/AC11, and the plan's carve-out preserves every §8 posture element the spec names: empty-reason clears, no signing/hashing, freely-removable panel for no-refutation beads), but the section-attribution and the adjective 'unchanged' now disagree between spec and plan; a Bead-3 AC6 check against the spec text would mis-grade the amendment. Prose reconciliation.

F3-R2-3 (round-2 blast-radius nit) ADDRESSED: Bead-2 step 6 now carries the corrected caller taxonomy — 'do NOT claim all best-effort', class (1) already-fatal (repair.go:86 guard.NewFailure + phase.EnsureMigrated propagating fatally from complete.go:283 and both approve paths — both re-verified in code), class (2) best-effort/warn (complete's doc-skew/adr-override/adr-supersede/panel audits + approve writes; approve/spec.go:74 and approve/plan.go:180 warn-append, consistent with the class even though not named), class (3) ignore (release.go:133 discards the return — verified '_ ='), with the per-class no-regression argument and the blast-radius grep added as a Bead-2 verification step and in Testing Strategy. Residual LOW typo: the plan writes the fatal caller as internal/bead/repair.go:86; the actual file is cmd/mindspec/repair.go:86 (repairMergeMetadataFn seam, cmd/mindspec/repair.go:22-25,84-93).

Durable-obligation additions introduce no contradiction with R1/R2/OQs/AC5/the message-superset design or the rest of the plan: (a) step-2.3 placement (after the 2.25 gate, before 2.5 CommitAll) plus step-3.75 (after the ADR-divergence gate complete.go:542-544, before close :546-547) yields the pending→satisfied→close ordering the invariant proof-sketch requires, and the already-closed recovery branch (:547-554, no verify-close) is correctly NOT leaned on — both writes precede the close, pinned by test 7(B)(vii); (b) the fail-open override fires only on a recorded unsatisfied obligation, so AC5's fail-open row and §6 dogfooding are preserved for every no-refutation bead, and the plain-pass row 7(B)(x) pins that no marker is written absent applied refutations; (c) refutations never alter the leg-9.5 Block message, so the verified round-2 superset/fixture analysis stands unchanged, and the AC10 no-refut predicate is scoped to the RC Block message — the obligation-refusal failure is a distinct guard message that advises restore-panel/restore-audit for an ALREADY-applied refutation, not a recording incantation for dodging an RC (no OQ4 conflict); (d) the durable-vs-best-effort asymmetry is grounded solely in AppliedRefutations, stated identically in step 5, Bead-2 AC, and Bead-3 step 3 — abandon/env-skip correctly remain merging Warn paths with no obligation. §8-amendment-vs-§6: COHERENT, not a contradiction — §6 fail-opens on the ABSENCE of gate inputs, while refutation_pending on bead metadata is a present, durable record on the state authority (ADR-0023's division of labor), so refusing is the fail-closed-with-evidence branch of §6's own rationale; the carve-out is narrowly scoped to audit durability (no signing/hashing, empty reason still clears, panel freely removable for no-refutation beads) and the plan states the §6 scoping identically in Bead-3 step 3, step 4, and ADR Fitness.
